title: Better Than Adventure! Modernized
description: This resource pack brings all of Better Than Adventure's textures and visuals into modern Minecraft!
body:
Better than Adventure! Modernized is a resource pack that brings the textures and visuals from the Beta 1.7.3 mod, Better than Adventure! 

This resource pack supports 1.21-1.21.4! 1.21.5 support coming soon!

BTA's official website:
[https://www.betterthanadventure.net](https://www.betterthanadventure.net)

# Features

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

* Non-directional textures! (May not work 50% of the time)

</details>

# Requirements
Place Programmer Art below the resource pack or it will not function properly! Here's what it looks like:
![Screenshot of Programmer Art placed below the resource pack itself.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bba9eeb479e9cb9f18aa9adbc541077c63ef5318.png)

# Addons
These are third-party mods/resource packs you can add to get a much better experience.

## Mods
[Nostalgic Tweaks](https://modrinth.com/mod/nostalgic-tweaks)

[Moderner Beta](https://modrinth.com/mod/moderner-beta)

[Animatium](https://modrinth.com/mod/animatium)

## Resource Packs
Place these on top of Programmer Art, but below BTA Modernized.

[Golden Days (Base)](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/golden-days)

[Programmer Art Fix](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/programmer-art-fix)

[Programmer Art Continuation Project (PACP)](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/pacp)

[Programmer Art Ultimate](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/programmer-art-ultimate)

[Programmer Art+](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/programmer-art-plus)

[Beta English](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/beta-english) (for old Beta translations!)

# FAQ
Q: There are missing texture glitches in blocks and items. How can I fix it?

A: Don't fix it yourself. It will mess up the resource pack more. Instead, report it in the #suggestions channel in the Discord server. I will fix it ASAP.

Q: Why is the beta version naming scheme 1.x.x.x?

A: This naming scheme was taken from Bedrock Edition (pre-1.16)'s naming scheme for betas or development versions. The format goes like this: major.minor.patch.revision. Get more info at: [https://minecraft.wiki/w/Version_formats#Pocket_Edition](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Version_formats#Pocket_Edition)

Q: How often are versions released?

A: Usually once or twice every week. Sometimes I upload versions on more days of the week than on others. Usually on holidays or vacations, development is paused until I get back home. (except for Christmas if I have time) Nightly builds are released, but they also won't release during out-of-town times.

Q: What is the terminology for versions?

A: Versions starting with 'alpha' means the version is in early development. Since this is a resource pack, there will be less bugs. Versions with 'beta' in front of the version number means that it's a beta build. Versions without a 1.x.x or 1.x.x.x means it's either a nightly build or a version with an unknown version number. Usually in these cases, a numerical build number will be the version number. (Format: yyyymmdd.revision or yyyymmdd.revision-nightly for nightly builds, e.g. 20250504.01 or 20250504.02-nightly)

Q: What are 'nightly builds'?

A: Nightly builds are usually builds compiled after a certain time. Nightly builds are usually compiled after 7:30 PM Pacific Time. Builds that were created before 7:30 PM and compiled after 7:30 at that same day are still nightly builds.

Q: Why are there modern textures in the resource pack?

A: Actually, it's not about the resource pack. To get all of the old textures, use the Programmer Art resource pack like shown on the top image.
